value_chain_position: Cloud data platform providing data warehousing, data sharing and AI/ML workload infrastructure — sits in the software-infrastructure layer that AI training and inference pipelines consume for data access and feature engineering.
relative_comps: SNOW's 31% 3-year CAGR and 31% YoY growth make it one of the faster-growing names in the chunk; net cash balance sheet and 23% FCF margin are strong for a consumption-model SaaS. The AI-infrastructure fit is real — Snowflake's data cloud is increasingly where enterprises stage data for LLM fine-tuning and inference. Vs. Databricks (private), SNOW competes on SQL/BI workloads while Databricks leads on ML engineering; both are required in a full AI data stack. Microsoft Fabric and AWS Redshift are the chief substitution risks. Defense_tech hint stripped.
